cataloging_subject.label: Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda of St Mary-at-Hill, 1420–1559 (Littlehales Chapters V–VI selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The exact bounded editorial unit encoded by ajt8135: the 1904 and 1905 edition title leaves [iii]–[iv] and Littlehales Chapters V–VI, printed pp. 61–413. It is not the whole two-part edition, one authored medieval work, either manuscript as a whole, or all surviving St Mary-at-Hill records. The inherited U-M source note says Chapters IV and V, but the body headings, table of contents, and continuous printed pagination identify Chapters V and VI.

form_statements.description: The accounts and memoranda are prose. Eight serialized L elements are two copies of a four-line Longfellow title-page epigraph and are editorial paratext, not intrinsic verse.

scope.description: Parish churchwardens’ accounts, memoranda, inventories, and related administrative record matter

subject_statements.label: The church as building, institution, and Christian community

scope.description: Burials, obits, chantries, and commemorative expenditure within the account selection

summaries.value: An edition-defined selection of late-medieval and early-modern parish churchwardens’ accounts and memoranda from St Mary-at-Hill in London. The records document receipts, expenditure, church fabric, rents, liturgical and commemorative activity, personnel, burials, inventories, and parish administration from 1420 to 1559. Littlehales printed MS A in full through 1494–95 and selected later matter while preserving an annual frame; one memorandum at p. 240 is explicitly supplied from MS B, leaf 62.

The electronic item joins bounded material from two distinct EETS carriers: 1904 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 61–356, followed by 1905 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 357–413. The XML serializes both title-page divisions before both body chapters. Printed pp. 1–60 and 414–449, unprinted manuscript matter, and all other P69/MRY4 records are outside the cataloging subject.

extent.description: Two intellectual sections, Chapters V and VI. No defensible intellectual-item total is assigned: 82 DIV2 account-year containers, 563 DIV3 containers, 724 P elements, 578 tables, and 5,861 rows are encoding/editorial metrics, and 33 subordinate DIV3 containers are explicit omission placeholders rather than records. The four content parts are non-count-bearing. Eight L elements are repeated title-page epigraph lines and do not count as intrinsic verse.

content_parts.label: Part I title-page paratext
content_parts.summary: The encoded title leaves of the 1904 EETS OS 125 carrier, including bibliographic matter, a record quotation, and a four-line Longfellow epigraph.
notes: Non-count-bearing editorial paratext; its four L elements do not change the prose form of the cataloged selection.

content_parts.label: V. Churchwardens’ Accounts
content_parts.summary: Churchwardens’ accounts from 1420 through the mid-1490s, printed from MS A and including receipts, payments, church fabric, rents, chantries, burials, inventories, and parish administration.
notes: MS A is the continuous source stream. The one explicitly scoped MS B memorandum occurs later in Chapter VI, at p. 240.

content_parts.label: Part II title-page paratext
content_parts.summary: The encoded title leaves of the distinct 1905 EETS OS 128 carrier, including bibliographic matter and a repeated four-line Longfellow epigraph.
notes: The XML places this title page in FRONT before both body chapters; that serialization does not make it historically precede Chapter V.

content_parts.label: VI. Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda
content_parts.summary: The continuation through 1559, selectively edited from MS A after 1495 while retaining the annual frame and distinct-value matter. It includes exactly one explicitly identified MS B contribution: the 1500–01 memorandum on p. 240 from MS B leaf 62, with a less explicit MS A parallel noted by Littlehales.

locators.value: ajt8135:4; PB 217–413; MS B memorandum at ajt8135:4.6.3, p. 240

notes: The chapter spans both printed carriers. The single MS B memorandum does not create a second whole-chapter transmission stream.

evidence.summary: The pinned XML supplies the exact item title, U-M header, source-edition statement, represented chapters and pages, historical responsibility, languages in the text, and the bounded encoded selection.

evidence.citation: Independent structural, page-boundary, omission, gap, and language audit of ajt8135.xml
evidence.locator: FRONT/BODY; DIV1–DIV3; PB/EPB; TABLE/ROW/CELL; GAP; NOTE1; L; language declarations and body text
evidence.summary: The XML contains two title-page DIV1 units and Chapters V–VI; 82 DIV2 and 563 DIV3 containers; 353 consecutive body PB values 61–413; 33 explicit subordinate omission placeholders; 41 GAP elements; and eight title-epigraph L elements. No Greek target text or body-level language assignment was found. These serialized counts are not intellectual-item counts.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Medieval records of a London city church, ajt8135
evidence.summary: The exact U-M item supplies the inherited discovery title, Chapter V–VI table of contents, source-edition context, current access, and current public-domain/No Copyright-US statement.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Henry Littlehales, Part I, EETS OS 125 (1904)
evidence.summary: The fixity-verified Part I carrier supports the 1904 title leaves, printed pp. 61–356, Chapter V, the first portion of Chapter VI, Littlehales’s editorial responsibility, and the p. 240 MS B memorandum.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Henry Littlehales, Part II, EETS OS 128 (1905)
evidence.summary: The fixity-verified Part II carrier supports the 1905 title leaves, printed pp. 357–413, the conclusion of Chapter VI, Littlehales’s MS A/MS B descriptions, and his selection and ordering method.

notes: The inherited U-M source note says Chapters IV and V; the encoded headings, contents, and pagination prove that the retained chapters are V and VI.

MS A is the sole continuous manuscript stream. MS B contributes only the explicitly scoped 1500–01 memorandum at p. 240, leaf 62; the less explicit MS A parallel is an editorial note, not a second transmitted text.
The historical one-volume MS A description and the three current TLA /001 components remain separate lineage evidence layers until an institutional concordance is obtained.
Witness holdings, component access, facsimile completeness, reproduction terms, and rights conflicts remain in the linked lineage manifest.
